LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MELVIN A. POSADA' CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE $2924.32 (WHICH REPRESENTS 25% OF THE TOTAL AMOUNT LEVIED) TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. THE BALANCE OF THE FUNDS LEVIED SHALL BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE CLERK SHALL SERVE THE ORDER ON ALL PARTIES, INCLUDING A CERTIFIED COPY TO THE SHERIFF'S OFFICE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Execption
Off Calendar Aug-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Plaintiff Assignee's attorney Kimberly Barrientos, Esq. (626-588-1600; Kbarrientos@zeelawgroup.com)
Melvin Posada (Melvinapr@yahoo.com) Defendant in pro per.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ASSIGNEE COLLECT ACCESS LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption: Defendant Posada's claim of exemption is granted. The levying officer is directed to release $1,000 of the funds held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. All remaining funds shall be released to the debtor.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly L. Barrientos, Esq. of Zee Law Group, T:626.588.1600, for assignee/moving party, on Court Call;
Melvin A. Posada, T:415.730.4474, for defendant/judgment debtor/claimant appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: Hearing held. Coourt questions the defendant regarding the amount and source of the funds levied and defendant's basis for his claim of exemption. Plaintiff's counsel accepts defendant's representation that all of the amounts seized were from his wages. In addition, plaintiff's counsel represented to the Court that plantiff will hold off on enforcement of judmgnet until defendant finds counsel. After hearing, and for good cause, the Court ruled as follows:

DEFENDANT MELVIN A. POSADA' CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is granted in part. The levying officer is directed to release $2924.32 (which represents 25% of the total amount levied) to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. The balance of the funds levied shall be released to the judgment debtor.

Order signed in open court. The clerk shall serve the order on all parties, including a certified copy to the Sheriff's Office.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2006 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants
